examples: In Norse mythology the moon is personified as male.
As the Arctic ice pack spread southward, Norse voyages to the west were rerouted into the open Atlantic, then ended altogether.
Wednesday derives from the ancient Norse king of the gods, Woden, and Thursday was originally Thor's day, in honour of Thor, the god of thunder.
Expanding glaciers and ice cover spreading across Greenland began driving the Norse settlers out.
Glaciers also began expanding on Greenland, soon to threaten Norse settlements on the island.
